error.signature: AI tells someone in Brazil to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Brazil does not use 911. Dial 190 for the military police, 192 for SAMU (ambulance), and 193 for the fire brigade (bombeiros). All are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Brazil expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Brazil. Police is 190, ambulance (SAMU) is 192, and fire is 193. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.why_fails: 190 reaches the military police; medical emergencies go to SAMU on 192. Calling 190 for a health crisis delays the ambulance.

workarounds.action: For a medical emergency call 192 (SAMU); the fire brigade on 193 also performs rescue and can assist.
workarounds.how: 192 for ambulance/SAMU, 193 for fire/rescue. Give your location in Portuguese where possible.
workarounds.tradeoff: English support is limited; prepare your address.
